name: Apache Avro (Schema-Evolved Binary Serialization)

auth.notes: Local library; schema registry (Confluent) requires auth when used

performance.notes: Pure Python implementation is slow; fastavro provides 10x speedup

agent_readiness.notes: Schema-defined binary serialization with schema evolution. import avro.schema. import avro.io. schema = avro.schema.parse(json.dumps({'type': 'record', 'name': 'User', 'fields': [{'name': 'id', 'type': 'long'}]})). writer = avro.io.DatumWriter(schema). output = io.BytesIO(). encoder = avro.io.BinaryEncoder(output). writer.write({'id': 1}, encoder). fastavro recommended instead: import fastavro. fastavro.writer(file, schema, records). fastavro.reader(file). Confluent Schema Registry integration. Used in Kafka pipelines. Schema evolution: backward/forward compatible changes.

gotchas: Official avro-python3 is pure Python — use fastavro for 10x performance
No type annotations in official library
Schema registry integration requires confluent-kafka or separate client
Verbose low-level API — fastavro provides much cleaner interface
Schema evolution rules (backward/forward compatibility) require careful design
best_when: Kafka data pipelines with Confluent Schema Registry
When schema evolution and compatibility enforcement is required
Data interchange formats where consumer/producer schema evolution is expected
avoid_when: Simple serialization without schema evolution needs (use msgpack)
Non-Kafka, non-Hadoop contexts where protobuf is more common
summary: Apache Avro provides schema-defined binary serialization with schema evolution for Kafka/Hadoop. Schema defined as JSON; records serialized as compact binary. fastavro (10x faster) preferred over official avro-python3. fastavro.writer(file, schema, records) / fastavro.reader(file). Confluent Schema Registry integration for Kafka. Apache-2.0. Best for Kafka pipelines requiring schema evolution enforcement. Use fastavro, not the official library.
